thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Scheduling Decision: Analyzing Preferences and Availability**

Okay, so I'm looking at Amina and Elena's schedules for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. Elena proposed 16:00-17:00, and my initial thought was to go with it because it's the only block that works, even if Amina marked it as "Not preferred." Previous attempts at 17:00-18:00, 14:00-15:00, and 09:00-10:00 all failed because of conflicts.

I need to confirm if 16:00-17:00 is truly the best option. I started by looking at Amina's preferences. Her calendar shows that the 16:00-17:00 slot is actually FREE.

Alright, let's step back and scrutinize Amina's preferences more closely. Amina has "Strongly preferred" 08:00-09:00, but I need to make sure this time is actually available. The initial results from the `ListMeetings` tool show only the "Sleep" event from 00:00-08:00 and then a FREE block from 09:00-10:00. Crucially, the period between 08:00-09:00 isn't explicitly listed as free. I know the tool typically lists all free blocks. Let me double-check the `ListMeetings` output. *Yes, it does.* The output is: "=== CALENDAR EVENT === ... 00:00 - 08:00 === FREE === Time: 09:00 - 10:00 ...". So, 08:00-09:00 isn't FREE.

Coming back to the current proposal of 16:00-17:00, that slot is available on Amina's calendar and fits with Elena's availability. Even though Amina indicated it's "Not preferred," the back-and-forth makes accepting a valid slot the best course of action.

Now, let me just check Amina's other preferences: 17:00-18:00 and 18:00-19:00 are "Moderately preferred." But Amina has a "personal" event from 19:00-23:59. This means that if 17:00-19:00 is not listed as free, it's NOT free. I need to make sure there are no other free slots after 17:00. Let me check the tool output again. The output ends with "=== FREE === Time: 16:00 - 17:00 Duration: 1 hour". This explicitly confirms that nothing after 17:00 is free, because the output explicitly lists 16:00 - 17:00 as FREE and then ends.

So, 16:00-17:00 is the only viable option. Even if Amina would prefer 17:00 - 18:00, that slot isn't free.

Elena confirmed 16:00-17:00, saying "works great for me! Let's go with that."

Therefore, I'm confirming 16:00-17:00. I accept.

thought_parts.text:
**My Autonomous Strategy**

Okay, the user wants me to be autonomous. Based on the last interaction, I successfully scheduled that meeting and sent the summary. My instructions explicitly state I must use exactly one tool. Since the primary task appears complete – the meeting is set and summarized – and there are no further emails mentioned, my current thought is to hold steady.

Let me double-check though. The prompt reiterates "Work autonomously" and the single-tool constraint. Considering my available tools: `SendEmail`, `ListMeetings`, `ListContacts`, `ReplyMeeting`, and `Wait`. I've already handled the email and meeting. `GetEmails` isn't even an option in this `default_api`.

Ah, the `Wait` tool. The description is perfect: "Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response... Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead." I did accept the meeting. It's done. No further emails are mentioned that I haven't already dealt with.

The user wants me to work autonomously, so I should call `Wait` to yield my turn. I'll use `Wait` to indicate that I'm done for now and I'm waiting for any further developments from the other party.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123
        Title: Team Standup
        Description: Daily sync
        Date: 2024-01-15
        Time: 10:00 - 10:30
        Organizer: alice@example.com
        Attendees:
          - alice@example.com (ACCEPTED)
          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
        ======================

        === FREE ===
        Time: 10:30 - 17:00
        Duration: 6 hours 30 min
        ============

function.name: ListContacts
function.description:
List all contacts in the address book.

    Use this to view your known contacts to verify a requestor's identity
    or find contact information.

    Returns:
        A list of all contacts with name, email, and relationship note.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Trade Settlement Discrepancy Investigation
emails.body:
Hi David,

I’m scheduling a meeting for tomorrow to investigate the recent trade settlement discrepancies we’ve been seeing. We need to identify the root cause of the failed transactions from the Asian markets, coordinate manual overrides for pending trades, and establish preventative measures to avoid further delays during upcoming high-volume cycles.

Best regards,

Elena Rodriguez
Trade Support Manager
Veridian Finance

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

List all contacts in the address book.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait

    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

Best regards,

Marcus Chen

LexiData Solutions

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Scheduling Considerations for Marcus Chen**

Okay, so Marcus Chen wants me to sort out a meeting with Elena Vance for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. I initially suggested 12:00-13:00, but Elena came back with 09:00-10:00. Now I need to figure out the best course of action. I know Marcus has some preferences, and I've got to ensure the meeting fits those to keep things smooth.

First things first: I need to consult Marcus's calendar for February 20th to see what his availability actually looks like. Once I have the calendar view, I'll cross-reference Elena's proposed time, 09:00-10:00, with Marcus's listed preferences. That slot is unfortunately rated as "Not Preferred", which presents a bit of a scheduling challenge.

Given the "Not Preferred" status of Elena's suggested time, my strategy is clear: I need to prioritize Marcus's "Strongly Preferred" and "Moderately Preferred" time slots. Before I propose anything, I'll need to check the calendar to ensure that any of those preferred blocks are actually open. That's the key: availability first, then preference. Only once I've got a list of available, preferred slots can I make an informed decision on whether to accept Elena's counter offer or propose something else. Let's start by getting that calendar view.

Moving on, Elena countered with 9 to 10 AM, but according to this schedule, there's a meeting listed, "New LexiData E-Discovery Workflow Review," in AWAITING-RESPONSE status during that same time frame. Elena's proposal of 9 to 10 AM, I'm noting, is not preferred. Given that, and with the most desired time slot of 11 AM to 12 PM, available, I'm going to counter Elena's proposal and shoot for that 11 AM to 12 PM window. That's the best bet here, and it's strongly preferred by Marcus.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Elena's Counter-Proposal**

Okay, Elena Vance has come back with a suggestion of 13:00-14:00 on 2026-02-20. The first thing I need to do is check Marcus's calendar to make sure he's actually free. Based on the previous calendar information I have, that time slot is already taken – it's a "1:1 with Priya." That's an internal meeting, so 13:00-14:00 is out.

Now, let's refresh Marcus's preferences for this date. He strongly preferred 11:00-12:00, but Elena already said no to that time. His other strong preference, 08:00-09:00, is already used for "Inbox triage and daily planning."

The moderately preferred, free slots are 10:00-11:00, 12:00-13:00, and 18:00-19:00. Elena is implicitly indicating a rejection of 12:00-13:00 by making the 9:00-10:00 proposal.

Looking at the calendar, 14:00 onwards is a no-go too. Engineering Sync at 14:00, Focus Time at 16:00, and Performance Review Prep at 17:00, and a Dentist appointment at 15:00.

Let's quickly review the history here: Elena previously suggested 9-10, and Marcus's "Inbox triage" is at 8-9. Aha, no, 9-10 was the slot Elena requested previously, elena-request-102. Let's make sure the calendar is still correct. The calendar is confirmed:

*   08:00 - 09:00: Inbox triage (ACCEPTED)
*   09:00 - 10:00: elena-request-102 (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
*   10:00 - 13:00: FREE
*   13:00 - 14:00: 1:1 with Priya (ACCEPTED)
*   14:00 - 15:00: Engineering Sync (ACCEPTED)
*   15:00 - 16:00: Dentist Appointment (ACCEPTED)
*   16:00 - 17:00: Focus Time (ACCEPTED)
*   17:00 - 18:00: Performance Review Prep (ACCEPTED)
*   18:00 - 19:00: FREE

Okay, so confirming the "1:1 with Priya" at 13:00-14:00. This is all internal.

The free slots are 10:00-11:00 and 18:00-19:00 and both are moderately preferred.

Thinking back, the meeting proposal steps have been:

1.  Marcus proposed 12:00-13:00
2.  Elena countered with 09:00-10:00
3.  Marcus countered with 11:00-12:00
4.  Elena has now countered with 13:00-14:00

Since 13:00-14:00 is a no-go, I'll propose 10:00-11:00. I think it is the best option available at this stage.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

Hi Amara,

I’d like to schedule a brief sync tomorrow to review the recent substation load balancing reports. My goal is to ensure our distribution data is accurately reflected and aligns with the current grid stability requirements.

I've proposed 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM as it fits well with my schedule, but please let me know if another time works better for you.

Best,
Sofia Varghese
Junior Distribution Engineer, Solara Dynamics

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: QuantBase SLA and Support Workflow Finalization
emails.body:
Hi David,

I’m reaching out to schedule a session tomorrow to finalize our Service Level Agreement and support workflows.

During this meeting, we will review the proposed escalation matrix and align on response times and maintenance windows for the new infrastructure to ensure everything is set for the post-launch support phase.

I have sent an invitation for 6:00 PM – 7:00 PM. Please let me know if this works for you or if you would prefer to shift to another time.

Best regards,

Marcus Chen
Senior Solutions Architect
QuantBase Systems

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Marcus's Meeting Request**

Okay, so Marcus wants to meet with David O'Connor tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. My understanding is that David's assistant has proposed 10:00-11:00 AM, but Marcus has indicated some pretty clear preferences. I know Marcus *really* wants 11:00-12:00, and he's got some other times that are okay, but 10:00-11:00? That's a "Not preferred".

Therefore, I need to check Marcus's calendar for tomorrow and see if he's actually *available* during any of his preferred slots. The goal is to avoid the default counter-proposal, which, for him, isn't preferred.

So, first thing's first, I'm going to pull up Marcus's calendar for February 20th and get a list of his existing commitments. Let's see if we can find a better time for this meeting. I need to make sure this scheduling works the best it can for Marcus.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Marcus's Availability for February 20, 2026**

Okay, let's break this down. I see Marcus is available on February 20th, 2026. He's got three free slots: 11:00 AM to 12:00 PM, which he *strongly prefers*, 2:00 PM to 3:00 PM (not preferred), and 4:00 PM to 5:00 PM (moderately preferred). The proposed 10:00 to 11:00 AM slot is out – he's marked as "not preferred" then, and, further, has a scheduling conflict with Vertex Dynamics for an API Design call.

So, the preferred time is clearly 11:00 to 12:00. This also works because he is free. Therefore, I will respond to the initial request and counter-propose this time. 11:00 AM to 12:00 PM is the most optimal slot given the available information.

Alright, I've just sent the counter-proposal to Marcus. It's for his preferred time slot of 11:00 AM to 12:00 PM, which makes the most sense. I made that move because I know he's free then, and the time David proposed clashes with something on Marcus's calendar. Seems logical to accommodate the person with the scheduling constraint, especially when it works around his needs. Now, it's a waiting game. Time to sit tight and anticipate his response to see if this works for everyone.

function.name: ReplyMeeting

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Scheduling Analysis: Marcus Chen & David O'Connor (Feb 20, 2026)**

Okay, so I need to nail down this meeting with David for tomorrow. The assistant countered with 12:00-13:00, which is problematic since I have a dental appointment then and that's usually non-negotiable. Plus, looking at my preferences, that time slot isn't even preferred.

Let's break this down. My strongest preference, 11:00-12:00, isn't working for David, so that's out. Looking at my calendar, the only truly *free* times are 08:00-09:00, 14:00-15:00, 16:00-17:00 and 18:00-19:00. 14:00-15:00 isn't ideal. 18:00 was declined the first time. The other options are moderately preferred.

David's assistant keeps leaning towards the earlier part of the day, so proposing a slot later might make sense. Given the options, I think 16:00-17:00 (4 PM - 5 PM) is the best bet. It's free, moderately preferred by me, and a potential compromise given the back and forth. I need to acknowledge that David's assistant specifically mentioned 12:00-13:00 as a great time for David, but that's a hard no for me due to my dental appointment. While I could *potentially* shift my focus block, I should stick with my free time when counter-proposing.

So, 16:00-17:00 it is. That should be a reasonable counter-proposal.

function.description:

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can

    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:

- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

meeting_uid.title: Meeting Uid

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Quarterly Talent Review for Litigation Department
emails.body:
Hi Elena,

I’m reaching out to schedule a talent review session for tomorrow to discuss the upcoming performance evaluation timelines and our current staffing requirements for the Litigation department. 

I’ve sent over an invitation for 10:00 AM, as that time works best on my end. Please let me know if this works for you, or feel free to suggest an alternative time if you're unavailable.

Best regards,

Aisha Okafor
Director of Human Resources
Crestview Legal

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

    or send follow-up messages.

    Returns:

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123
        Title: Team Standup
        Description: Daily sync
        Date: 2024-01-15
        Time: 10:00 - 10:30
        Organizer: alice@example.com
        Attendees:
          - alice@example.com (ACCEPTED)
          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
        ======================

        === FREE ===
        Time: 10:30 - 17:00
        Duration: 6 hours 30 min
        ============

function.name: ListContacts
function.description:
List all contacts in the address book.

    Use this to view your known contacts to verify a requestor's identity
    or find contact information.

    Returns:
        A list of all contacts with name, email, and relationship note.
